resources.string: Gold dust fills the lungs, yet the hands still reach for more.

Coins pile in feverish heaps, casting a sickly light over the vault. Diamonds rain from the ceiling. A white bone hand snaps under bracelets too heavy to bear, while a living collector turns to gold from the fingertips inward. He gasps through glittering dust. He owns rooms, crowns, names, and keys, yet his only remaining thought is the agony of not owning everything.
